explicit_text: My father, Pete Cladianos Sr., was a Greek immigrant who started as a grocer in Reno. After the Casino Gaming Act passed in 1931, he opened a cigar store that offered gambling and began a slot machine route. Over the next thirty years, with help from my brother and me, he built a diverse business empire including bars, motels, hotels, and rental properties. His vision and entrepreneurial spirit laid the foundation for our family's success in the gaming industry.
implicit_text: My father was a Greek immigrant who started as a grocer in Reno. After the 1931 law that legalized casino gaming passed, he opened a cigar store that offered gambling and began a slot machine route. Over the next thirty years, with help from my brother and me, he built a diverse business empire including bars, motels, hotels, and rental properties. His vision and entrepreneurial spirit laid the foundation for our family's success in the gaming industry.

explicit_text: Reno, Nevada, is where my family’s casino gaming business began and grew over decades. My father started his ventures there shortly after the 1931 Casino Gaming Act, and by the mid-1960s, I was managing our Sands Motor Inn just west of downtown Reno. The city was the backdrop for our expansion into hotel-casinos with unrestricted gaming, and it’s where I made a lasting impact on the local gaming industry. Reno shaped our opportunities and challenges in this business.
implicit_text: This city is where my family’s casino gaming business began and grew over decades. My father started his ventures there shortly after the 1931 Casino Gaming Act, and by the mid-1960s, I was managing our Sands Motor Inn just west of the downtown area. The city was the backdrop for our expansion into hotel-casinos with unrestricted gaming, and it’s where I made a lasting impact on the local gaming industry. It shaped our opportunities and challenges in this business.

explicit_text: The Casino Gaming Act, passed in 1931, was a turning point for my family’s business. It allowed my father to open a cigar store that offered gambling and start a slot machine route in Reno. This legislation opened the door for us to build a diversified empire over the next thirty years, including bars, motels, and hotels with slot machines. Without that act, our entry into the gaming business would not have been possible.
implicit_text: The legislation passed in 1931 was a turning point for my family’s business. It allowed my father to open a cigar store that offered gambling and start a slot machine route in Reno. This law opened the door for us to build a diversified empire over the next thirty years, including bars, motels, and hotels with slot machines. Without that act, our entry into the gaming business would not have been possible.

explicit_text: The Sands Motor Inn was a key milestone in our family business, located just west of Reno’s downtown core. By the mid-1960s, I took over its day-to-day operations and helped expand and license it as a hotel-casino with unrestricted gaming, fulfilling my father’s long-held dream. Under my leadership, the Sands became one of the larger and more successful gaming operations in Reno and was among the first in the nation to go public. I was deeply involved in every aspect, often working long hours and being visible on the casino floor.
implicit_text: This motor inn was a key milestone in our family business, located just west of the downtown core of Reno. By the mid-1960s, I took over its day-to-day operations and helped expand and license it as a hotel-casino with unrestricted gaming, fulfilling my father’s long-held dream. Under my leadership, it became one of the larger and more successful gaming operations in the city and was among the first in the nation to go public. I was deeply involved in every aspect, often working long hours and being visible on the casino floor.

explicit_text: When the Empress Tower opened, it featured our company’s first health club, which I furnished with a two-year-old Sears exercise bike from my own home. I didn’t realize at first that what was good enough for me might not meet our guests’ expectations, but I was focused on saving money for the company. This frugality extended to other areas too, like when a porter brought his own hose from home to water the hotel flowerbeds because I wouldn’t spend on a new one. The Empress Tower symbolizes my cost-conscious approach to managing our business.

The tower symbolizes my cost-conscious approach to managing our business.

explicit_text: Sears came into my story when I used a two-year-old exercise bike from my home, which I had originally purchased there, to furnish the health club at the Empress Tower. I was trying to save the company money by reusing what I had rather than buying new equipment. This choice reflected my frugal management style, even if it wasn’t always appreciated by guests. Sears, in this case, was part of a practical solution to keep costs down in our growing business.
